folio.label: 17v
folio.l: Euäe in his lyue , by day or nygút o stounde
In ony contre , of this Theseus
And he were caugút , it was a corded thus
That w a swerd , he shulde lese hise hede
There was non oæä remedy , ne rede
But taketh his leue , and homward he hi~ spedde
Lat hi~ be ware , his nekke lith to wedde
So gret a sorwe , suffreth now Arcite
The deth he feleth , æurugú his hert smyte
He wepeth and wayleth , he crieth pytously
To sle hi~ self , he wayteth preuyly
He seide allas , the day æat he was born~
Now is my prison wers , than biforn~
Now is me shape , eternely to dwelle

note.notetxt: 367There is a cross in dry point in the left margin. To indicate what? if Dd as copytext, mark of text copied? or something that the scribe had to do there.ODR

folio.l: Nought in purgatorie , but in helle
Allas æ euäe I knew , Parotheus
For elles had I dwelled , w Duke Theseus
Fetered in his prisou~ , for euäe mo
Than had I ben in blisse , & nou©t in woo
Only the sigút of hire , whom æat I serue
Thougú æ I neuäe , hire gce may deserue
Wold haue suffised , rigút I now to me
O . dere Cosyn , Palamon , q¶ he
Thyn is the victorie , of this auenture
Ful blisfully in prisou~ , maist æè endure
In prisou~ , nay certes , but in Paradys
Wel hath fortune , turned the the dys
That hast the sigút , of hire , & I the absence
For possible it is , sithe æè hast hire päsence
And art a knygút , a worthy & an able
That be som cas , sithe fortune is chaungeable
Thow maist to thy desire , som tyme atteyne
But I æ am exiled , and barayne
Of alle grace , and in so gret dispeyre
That there nys no Water , Fyr , ne Eyre
Ne creature , that of hem maked Is
That may me hele , or do comfort in this
Wel ought I sterue , in wanhope & distresse
Fare wel my lyf , my lust , and my gladnesse
¢ Allas why pleyne so folk , in comune
On purueance of god , or of fortune
